ps140: O venerable father John,* furnishing your mind with wings to fly to God with faith,* you disdained the disorderly state of the world* and, taking up your cross,* you followed after Him who beholds all things,* enslaving to your mind the intemperance of your body* through feats of asceticism,* by the power of the divine Spirit.

O venerable John, you found Gregory to be like a sun of great radiance,* shining with the light of the virtues* and the effulgence of miracles,* and you illumined the eyes of your soul,* dispelled the dark passions, and became a light;* and rejoicing, O all-blessed father,*you passed over to the never-waning Light.

You spent your whole life in great stillness, in weeping and tears,* becoming a true monk by faith,* adorned with non-acquisitiveness* and made beautiful through abstinence,* showing yourself to be a stranger and a sojourner on earth.* Wherefore, O right wondrous one,* you have found the delight of the good.
theotokion: The pre-eternal God, receiving flesh through your blood,* has shown you to be an intercessor for men, O pure one.* Wherefore, deliver your servants from all misfortune and every evil circumstance,* and from the snares of the wicked enemy,* and vouchsafe that all who glorify and do you homage* may share in the splendour of the elect.
stavrotheotokion: “What is this sight which I see, which my eyes behold, O Master?* You who sustain all creation has been suspended upon the Tree,* and You die, giving life to all!”* said the Theotokos, weeping,* when she beheld, uplifted upon the Cross,* You, the God and Man* who ineffably shone forth from her.
